question: How does Temperature vary across different Spectral Classes?

analysis.design_rationale: A strip plot (scatter with jitter along the categorical x-axis) gives a granular, detailed view of how individual stars' Temperature (K) values are distributed by Spectral Class. This design lets us spot clustering, gaps, and trends that might be hidden in summary statistics. By using vertical jitter/scatter on x-axis, each point represents a star, showing density and possible multimodality per class. Color encoding can highlight temperature gradients, adding another layer of insight to spot differences across Spectral Classes.
analysis.target_insight: Shows the actual distribution of temperature values per Spectral Class and reveals density, clusters, and outliers, as well as overall temperature gradient across the spectral types.

chart_encodings.other_aesthetics: Jitter added to X-position for readability; transparency (alpha) for overlapping points; colorbar for temperature; ordered x-axis; clean axis labeling

transformations: Sort df by Spectral Class according to standard sequence: O, B, A, F, G, K, M
Convert Spectral Class to ordered categorical
Map Temperature (K) to color for scatter plot
Add small random jitter to X-axis numeric positions for each Spectral Class to avoid overplotting
data_preparation_steps: Convert df['Spectral Class'] to ordered categorical
Assign numeric X positions to Spectral Classes (e.g., 0 to 6) for plotting
Add random jitter to each X position for visual separation
suggestion.matplotlib_code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib as mpl
df = pd.read_csv('csv_c_squared\192.csv')
ordered_classes = ['O','B','A','F','G','K','M']
df['Spectral Class'] = pd.Categorical(df['Spectral Class'], categories=ordered_classes, ordered=True)
df = df.sort_values('Spectral Class')
class_to_num = {cl:i for i,cl in enumerate(ordered_classes)}
x_vals = df['Spectral Class'].map(class_to_num).values
jitter = np.random.uniform(-0.18, 0.18, size=len(x_vals))
x_pos = x_vals + jitter
temps = df['Temperature (K)'].values
cmap = mpl.colormaps['plasma']
norm = mpl.colors.Normalize(vmin=temps.min(), vmax=temps.max())
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10,6))
sc = ax.scatter(x_pos, temps, c=temps, cmap=cmap, norm=norm, alpha=0.72, edgecolor='none', s=38)
ax.set_xticks(range(len(ordered_classes)))
ax.set_xticklabels(ordered_classes, fontsize=12)
ax.set_xlabel('Spectral Class', fontsize=13)
ax.set_ylabel('Temperature (K)', fontsize=13)
ax.set_title('Individual Star Temperatures by Spectral Class\n(Strip Plot: Jittered Scatter, Color by Temperature)', fontsize=15)
cbar = plt.colorbar(sc, ax=ax, orientation='vertical', pad=0.01)
cbar.set_label('Temperature (K)', fontsize=12)
ax.grid(axis='y', linestyle='--', alpha=0.36)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
